original_abstract: Rapid advancements in model scaling have enabled large language models (LLMs) to achieve remarkable zero-shot performance across a diverse array of natural language processing (NLP) tasks. The emergence of ChatGPT, in particular, has captivated the research community through its ability to generate sophisticated, human-like responses and iteratively self-correct based on conversational context. Despite its popularity, the extent to which ChatGPT functions as a truly versatile generalist across the broader NLP landscape remains largely unexplored. In this study, we provide a comprehensive empirical evaluation of ChatGPT’s zero-shot capabilities across 20 hallmark datasets spanning seven distinct task categories. Our findings highlight both the strengths and inherent boundaries of the current model; while ChatGPT excels in areas requiring complex reasoning, such as arithmetic logic, it continues to struggle with specialized tasks like sequence tagging. Supplementing our quantitative results, we offer a qualitative analysis of specific case studies to provide deeper insight into the model's behavior and limitations.
humanized_abstract: Recent advances in model scaling have led to many large language models (LLMs) performing extremely well in many natural language processing (NLP) tasks in a zero-shot manner. ChatGPT is one of the recent releases that that has caused quite a buzz among researchers, due to the quality of its outputs, and ability to think and learn during a conversation through a sequence of refinement responses. The extent to which ChatGPT functions as a truly all-purpose general-purpose NLP language model remains unknown. In this work, we release the first extensive empirical evaluation for ChatGPT on the 20 popular benchmark datasets across seven popular NLP tasks, in a zero-shot manner. We report on ChatGPT’s strengths and weaknesses across the range of NLP tasks and detail its shortcomings on some benchmark tasks, while also exemplifying successful use cases on others. A set of qualitative case studies is used to gain more insights to the working and weaknesses of the ChatGPT language model.
